_description: JitNeuro workspace registry. Maps credentials, services, and integrations to their storage locations. This file contains ZERO secrets -- only pointers to .env files and vault references. Safe to commit.

vaults._description: Credential storage systems. Each vault has a type, auth method, and migration status. Credentials reference a vaultTarget to indicate where they should eventually live.

akv-prod.name: kv-yourcompany-prod
akv-prod.purpose: Production secrets (API keys, tokens, connection strings)

keeper.purpose: Personal credentials, PATs, SSH keys, browser-managed secrets

credentials._description: Each entry points to the .env file and key names where the secret lives. The secret itself is NEVER stored here. Fields: envFile (path to .env), keyNames (array of var names), purpose (what it does), vaultTarget (where it should migrate to), migrated (boolean), expires (ISO date, optional).

github_pat.purpose: GitHub API access and MCP integration

database.notes: Never use sa or root accounts. Create dedicated service accounts.

oauth_example._description: Example of an OAuth credential with detailed metadata. Include auth flow, scopes, and token refresh notes.
oauth_example.authFlow: OAuth2 authorization_code (one-time browser consent, then refresh tokens)
oauth_example.notes: Refresh tokens expire after 90 days of inactivity.

vault_migrated_example._description: Example of a credential that has been migrated to a vault. Claude reads the vault instead of the .env file when migrated=true.
vault_migrated_example.purpose: Example of a fully vault-migrated credential

envFiles._description: Maps every .env file location to its purpose. Claude checks this to find the right .env file. The central .env should be checked FIRST for most credentials.
envFiles.${WORKSPACE}/Automation/.env: Central credentials (API keys, tokens). Primary source -- check here FIRST.

services._description: Infrastructure services with per-environment URLs and health check endpoints. Claude uses these to test connectivity and route API calls to the correct environment.

automation-host._description: Example of a non-HTTP service (e.g., a Docker host, VM, or bare-metal server). Include SSH and relevant ports.

domains._description: DNS and hosting configuration. Track which domains exist, where DNS is managed, and any redirect relationships.

sendingDomains._description: Email sending domain configuration. Each entry maps a sending domain to its provider and purpose.

socialAccounts._description: Social media and public profile links. No secrets here -- just URLs for reference.

team._description: Team members with roles. Used for commit attribution and access context.

tools._description: Custom scripts and utilities with their paths and dependencies. Claude can invoke these directly when needed.
